channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: Southern California company wants to bring daylight to dark parts of Earth - KTLA
item.description:
1. Southern California company wants to bring daylight to dark parts of Earth  KTLA
  2. F.C.C. Approves Test of Space Mirror to Light Night Sky Despite Outcry  The New York Times
  3. FCC approves first Reflect Orbital satellite  SpaceNews
  4. FCC approves orbital space mirrors, first test satellites will launch this year — large spacecraft reflects sunlight to Earth’s surface for construction sites, search-and-rescue lighting, and more  Tom's Hardware
  5. U.S. regulator green-lights mission to launch giant mirror to orbit  The Hindu

item.title: Physicists recreate black hole energy extraction in the lab - ScienceDaily
item.description:
1. Physicists recreate black hole energy extraction in the lab  ScienceDaily

  3. Black hole theory: Synthetic ultrafast rotation amplifies electromagnetic waves  Open Access Government
  4. Physicists Replicate Black Hole Energy Extraction Using Synthetic Rotation  Tech Times
  5. CUNY physicists recreate black hole energy extraction in a historic lab experiment  The Brighter Side of News

item.title: The rivers on Titan flow, pool into lakes and are fed by rainfall on a slow cycle much like our own, but the liquid is methane and the ground beneath it is water frozen hard as stone - Space Daily
item.description:
1. The rivers on Titan flow, pool into lakes and are fed by rainfall on a slow cycle much like our own, but the liquid is methane and the ground beneath it is water frozen hard as stone  Space Daily
  2. Titan has rivers, rainfall, lakes and a slow hydrological cycle exactly like Earth's, except that every drop of it is liquid methane and the rock the rivers run over is water ice  Space Daily

item.title: Gravitational waves reveal hidden populations within black hole mergers - Phys.org
item.description: Gravitational waves reveal hidden populations within black hole mergers  Phys.orgSee more headlines & perspectives on Google News

item.title: A single rare meteorite has revealed the chemical fingerprint of a Moon-sized world that once orbited the young Sun before being destroyed in a catastrophic collision — a lost planet science never knew existed until it landed as a rock - Space Daily
item.description: A single rare meteorite has revealed the chemical fingerprint of a Moon-sized world that once orbited the young Sun before being destroyed in a catastrophic collision — a lost planet science never knew existed until it landed as a rock  Space Daily

item.title: This MIT robot flies through the air then dives underwater using the same wings - TechSpot
item.description:
1. This MIT robot flies through the air then dives underwater using the same wings  TechSpot
  2. New flapping robot swims and flies like a diving bird  MIT News
  3. This 250-Gram Robot Can Swim Underwater Then Fly into the Sky Using Its Flapping Wings Like a Diving Bird  ZME Science
  4. MIT researchers study avian mechanics to build robot that can dive, swim and fly  Silicon Republic
  5. [Science Through Covers] MIT winged amphibious robot leaps from water into the air  동아사이언스

item.title: James Webb has caught at least five galaxies colliding about 800 million years after the Big Bang, flinging heavy elements out into space far earlier than any model predicted — evidence the early universe was already messy and mature when it should still ha - Space Daily
item.description:
1. James Webb has caught at least five galaxies colliding about 800 million years after the Big Bang, flinging heavy elements out into space far earlier than any model predicted — evidence the early universe was already messy and mature when it should still ha  Space Daily
  2. What the huge young galaxies seen by JWST tell us about the universe  New Scientist
  3. James Webb telescope's largest-ever map of the universe unmasks hidden corners  Live Science

item.title: 1 month until the total solar eclipse 2026 — Here's what you need to know - Space
item.description:
1. 1 month until the total solar eclipse 2026 — Here's what you need to know  Space

  4. 10 best Spanish cities to see the total solar eclipse 2026  Space

  5. Pleiades star cluster, crescent moon seen in Heilongjiang, NE China  People's Daily Online

item.title: Earth’s inner core is not rock but a sphere of solid iron-rich metal, smaller than the Moon yet probably more massive. As the planet gradually loses heat, liquid metal freezes onto its surface, expanding it by roughly a millimetre each year. - Space Daily
item.description: Earth’s inner core is not rock but a sphere of solid iron-rich metal, smaller than the Moon yet probably more massive. As the planet gradually loses heat, liquid metal freezes onto its surface, expanding it by roughly a millimetre each year.  Space Daily

item.title: Scientists discover new seafloor being born — for the first time in history: ‘Massive event’ - New York Post
item.description:
1. Scientists discover new seafloor being born — for the first time in history: ‘Massive event’  New York Post
  2. In a First, Scientists Observe Creation of New Seafloor  The New York Times
  3. Anatomy of a seafloor spreading event captured by in situ seismogeodesy  Nature
  4. Scientists just caught a glimpse of Earth’s biggest game of The Floor Is Lava  Scientific American
  5. World First: Scientists Witnessed a Piece of Earth's Oceanic Crust Being Born  ScienceAlert

item.title: 'The moon looked wrong': Artemis II mission controller Chris White on taking historic lunar flyby photos from 250,000 miles away - Live Science
item.description: 'The moon looked wrong': Artemis II mission controller Chris White on taking historic lunar flyby photos from 250,000 miles away  Live Science

item.title: Voyager 1 Has Traveled Through Space at 38,000 Mph Since 1977 And Still Hasn't Reached 1 Light-Day - ScienceAlert
item.description:
1. Voyager 1 Has Traveled Through Space at 38,000 Mph Since 1977 And Still Hasn't Reached 1 Light-Day  ScienceAlert
  2. NASA is keeping Voyager 1 alive by switching off instruments and heaters one by one—but it cannot let the spacecraft become so cold that its fuel lines freeze. If those lines fail, Voyager could lose the thrusters that keep its antenna aimed at Earth, permanently  Space Daily
  3. Voyager 1 was launched in 1977 with a suite of 10 science instruments — as of 2026, only two remain switched on, and the spacecraft's nuclear power source loses roughly 4 watts every year, a slow drain that is now deciding what humanity still gets to learn fr  ScienceBlog.com
  4. Voyager Spacecraft: The Ultimate Power Management Challenge?  EE Times
  5. One Light-Day Away: Voyager's Epic Voyage  Rediff

item.title: A Jupiter-size planet that escaped its star’s death - Ars Technica
item.description:
1. A Jupiter-size planet that escaped its star’s death  Ars Technica
  2. Astronomers scrutinize exoplanet that survived the death of its star  Reuters
  3. Reuters Science News Summary  Devdiscourse
  4. Planet Seven Times Larger Than Its Star Survived a Sun’s Red Giant Phase  autoevolution
  5. A planet that shouldn’t exist: Webb telescope reveals the strange survival of WD 1856 b  Tech Explorist

item.title: An asteroid struck somewhere north of Australia 11 million years ago, scattering molten glass across a 900-kilometre ellipse in South Australia — yet scientists still cannot find the crater it left behind - Space Daily
item.description: An asteroid struck somewhere north of Australia 11 million years ago, scattering molten glass across a 900-kilometre ellipse in South Australia — yet scientists still cannot find the crater it left behind  Space Daily

item.title: When Italian and German researchers modeled a square-meter array of 1,482 neodymium magnets, the simulation showed roughly a fifth of incoming low-energy solar protons being deflected — suggesting a potential way for deep-space crews to reduce som - Space Daily
item.description: When Italian and German researchers modeled a square-meter array of 1,482 neodymium magnets, the simulation showed roughly a fifth of incoming low-energy solar protons being deflected — suggesting a potential way for deep-space crews to reduce som  Space Daily

item.title: Scientists Say They’ve Identified an Earth-Like Planet Right Next Door - Yahoo
item.description:
1. Scientists Say They’ve Identified an Earth-Like Planet Right Next Door  Yahoo
  2. Alien life on nearby exoplanet more likely than previously thought  The Jerusalem Post
  3. In November 2025, UC Irvine astronomers announced a rocky super-Earth called GJ 251 c orbiting in a habitable zone less than 20 light-years away — close enough that the next generation of ground-based telescopes may actually photograph it directly  Space Daily
  4. Nearby "Super Earth" Could Host Life After All  Universe Today
  5. This nearby exoplanet reignites hope: new signs of possible alien life detected  Futura, le média qui explore le monde

item.title: Vantablack: World's Blackest Paint Could Solve A Major Problem For Astronomy - ScienceAlert
item.description:
1. Vantablack: World's Blackest Paint Could Solve A Major Problem For Astronomy  ScienceAlert
  2. Ultra-Black Coating Could Mitigate Light Pollution Caused by Satellites  Universe Today
  3. Vantablack, the world's blackest paint, is shown to have the potential to prevent light pollution from satellites.  GIGAZINE
  4. Ultra-Black Coating Reduces Satellite Glare  Optics & Photonics News

item.title: If you compressed the entire history of Earth into a single calendar year, humans appear at 11:36 pm on the 31st of December, agriculture at 11:59, and everything written down in history occupies the final 15 seconds before midnight - Space Daily
item.description: If you compressed the entire history of Earth into a single calendar year, humans appear at 11:36 pm on the 31st of December, agriculture at 11:59, and everything written down in history occupies the final 15 seconds before midnight  Space Daily

item.title: Nearly a millennium ago sky-watchers recorded a new star so bright it was visible in daylight for weeks, and Hubble has now traced how far its remnant, the Crab Nebula, has expanded since — a thousand-year-old explosion still visibly unfolding - Space Daily
item.description: Nearly a millennium ago sky-watchers recorded a new star so bright it was visible in daylight for weeks, and Hubble has now traced how far its remnant, the Crab Nebula, has expanded since — a thousand-year-old explosion still visibly unfolding  Space Daily

item.title: AI Just Uncovered a Hidden Secret Inside Water - SciTechDaily
item.description:
1. AI Just Uncovered a Hidden Secret Inside Water  SciTechDaily
  2. Scientists used AI to crack one of water's biggest mysteries  ScienceDaily
  3. Machine learning evaluation of structural descriptors for supercooled water  Nature
  4. 💧 Water would be made of two liquids in one  Techno-Science.net

item.title: In March 2026, a strange 'chirp' from a distant dying star proved to be something never seen so clearly before: the birth of a magnetar — a newborn neutron star so magnetic it could wipe every credit card on Earth from halfway to the Moon - Space Daily
item.description: In March 2026, a strange 'chirp' from a distant dying star proved to be something never seen so clearly before: the birth of a magnetar — a newborn neutron star so magnetic it could wipe every credit card on Earth from halfway to the Moon  Space Daily

item.title: In May 1986, Leonid Kizim and Vladimir Solovyov left the newly opened Mir, crossed 2,500 kilometres to Salyut 7, and returned carrying 350 to 400 kilograms of instruments — the only human voyage ever made from one space station to another - Space Daily
item.description: In May 1986, Leonid Kizim and Vladimir Solovyov left the newly opened Mir, crossed 2,500 kilometres to Salyut 7, and returned carrying 350 to 400 kilograms of instruments — the only human voyage ever made from one space station to another  Space Daily

item.title: Science news this week: Time emerges inside a mini-universe, scientists thicken Arctic ice, and one of the oldest graves of a free Black person in the US found - Live Science
item.description:
1. Science news this week: Time emerges inside a mini-universe, scientists thicken Arctic ice, and one of the oldest graves of a free Black person in the US found  Live Science
  2. Physicists created a tiny universe where time emerged without a clock  ScienceDaily
  3. What if time isn't real? Physicists built a tiny quantum universe with 24,000 ultracold atoms and found a shocking truth: Time could create itself  The Times of India
  4. Physicists Watched Time Invent Itself Inside a Cloud of 24,000 Ultracold Atoms  TechEBlog -
  5. 24,000 Cold Atoms Model ‘Mini Universe’ Without A Ticking Clock  Quantum Zeitgeist
